pi_items.verified_value: Calculated volume using disk method with quadratic polynomial through the three points yields approximately 363,137 m³
pi_items.source: Numerical integration of disk method: V = π ∫[0 to 114] f(x)² dx where f(x) = 0.002518x² - 0.4493x + 46

pi_items.verified_value: Using verified Olympic pool volume of 2,500 m³ and calculated tower volume of 363,137 m³: capacity = 363,137/2,500 = 145.3 pools
pi_items.source: Calculated from verified volume; Olympic pool spec from https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Olympic-size_swimming_pool

pi_items.verified_value: 2500 m³ - Standard Olympic pool dimensions: 50m × 25m × 2m = 2,500 m³

privileged_info_summary: The task models the Drax Power Station cooling tower (114 meters high) based on three profile points. Base radius of 46m matches actual Drax base radius (43-46m from 86-92m diameter). Top height of 114m and top radius of 27.5m closely match Drax specifications (height 114m, top radius 26.5m from 53m diameter). However, the metadata volume range (390,000-420,000 m³) and pool range (150-170 pools) are contradicted by numerical computation. Using the disk method integral with a quadratic polynomial fitted to the three given points yields a volume of approximately 363,137 m³, equivalent to 145.3 Olympic pools. This is 26,863 m³ (about 7%) below the claimed minimum volume and 4.7 pools below the claimed minimum pool capacity. The Olympic swimming pool standard volume of 2,500 m³ is verified.
